repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
<!--
  Thank you for contributing to Neovim!
  If this is your first time, check out https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#pull-requests-prs
  for our PR guidelines.
-->

## Problem
Related issue #39239, restart cmd don't reset the cwd to where it startup, and don't drop the `[file]` arguments when pass parameter to new process.
## Solution
Add a global var `initialdir` store the directory and pass it at `channel_job_start`, use `if(strequal(...))` to match options that need value right behind it to filter `[file]`(as maintainer pointed out in the issue, it duplicate the scan `command_line_scan` have done, but i didn't work it out how to leverage that. Need some advice here.)
### AI assistance
Generate part of test file

comment_data.body: the new tests should live where the existing :restart tests live
comment_data.diff_hunk:

+local t = require('test.testutil')

+local tt = require('test.functional.testterm')
+
+local eq = t.eq
+local feed_data = tt.feed_data
+local retry = t.retry
+local is_os = t.is_os
+local new_pipename = n.new_pipename
+
+local env_notermguicolors = { COLORTERM = 'xterm-256color' }
+
+describe(':restart', function()

comment_data.body:
the ':cd should..." message makes this test failure harder to read. If that message were simply dropped, we could see the "actual" value instead of only the "expected" value. 

```
2026-05-04T13:36:21.0898421Z [1m[35mFAILED  [0m[0m [36m...est_xdg_ex_cmds/test/functional/ex_cmds/restart_spec.lua[0m @ [36m17[0m: [1m:restart use initial directory[0m
2026-05-04T13:36:21.0898607Z :cd should change cwd to D:/a/neovim/neovim/build/Xtest_tmpdir_ex_cmds/T282.2
2026-05-04T13:36:21.0898672Z stack traceback:
2026-05-04T13:36:21.0899029Z 	...est_xdg_ex_cmds/test/functional/ex_cmds/restart_spec.lua:50: in function <...est_xdg_ex_cmds/test/functional/ex_cmds/restart_spec.lua:17>
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,147 @@
+local t = require('test.testutil')
+local n = require('test.functional.testnvim')()
+local tt = require('test.functional.testterm')
+
+local eq = t.eq
+local feed_data = tt.feed_data
+local retry = t.retry
+local is_os = t.is_os
+local new_pipename = n.new_pipename
+
+local env_notermguicolors = { COLORTERM = 'xterm-256color' }
+
+describe(':restart', function()
+  before_each(n.clear)
+  after_each(n.check_close)
+
+  it('use initial directory', function()
+    local server_session
+    local tmpdir = t.tmpname()
+    finally(function()
+      if server_session then
+        server_session:close()
+      end
+      os.remove(tmpdir)
+    end)
+
+    local server_pipe = new_pipename()
+    local screen = tt.setup_child_nvim({
+      '-u',
+      'NONE',
+      '-i',
+      'NONE',
+      '--listen',
+      server_pipe,
+      '--cmd',
+      'set notermguicolors',
+    }, { env = env_notermguicolors })
+
+    -- Wait for TUI to be ready before feeding keys.
+    screen:expect({ any = 'TERMINAL' })
+
+    retry(nil, nil, function()
+      server_session = n.connect(server_pipe)
+    end)
+    local _, init_cwd = server_session:request('nvim_call_function', 'getcwd', {})
+    os.remove(tmpdir)
+    server_session:request('nvim_call_function', 'mkdir', { tmpdir, 'p' })
+    server_session:request('nvim_command', 'cd ' .. tmpdir)
+    local _, after_cd = server_session:request('nvim_call_function', 'getcwd', {})
+    eq(tmpdir, after_cd, ':cd should change cwd to ' .. tmpdir)

comment_data.body: as mentioned in https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/39239 , this is not the right approach

Yes, but i just can figure out a better idea, extract the logical from `command_line_scan` seem difficult, and the method i mention in #39239 may no meet your need, would you give me some advise or code snippets so i can know what to do?

don't need to extract logic from `command_line_scan`. ideas:

- `set_argf_var` already decides which args are "file args"
- or, `command_line_scan` could set a hint which :restart can use later

why not update the existing test on line 495?

also add some files in between arguments, to test more edge cases
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -554,6 +554,130 @@ describe('TUI :restart', function()
     screen:expect({ any = vim.pesc('[Process exited 0]') })
   end)
 
+  it('use initial directory', function()
+    local server_session
+    local tmpdir = t.tmpname(false)
+    finally(function()
+      if server_session then
+        server_session:close()
+      end
+      vim.uv.fs_rmdir(tmpdir)
+    end)
+
+    local server_pipe = new_pipename()
+    local screen = tt.setup_child_nvim({
+      '-u',
+      'NONE',
+      '-i',
+      'NONE',
+      '--listen',
+      server_pipe,
+      '--cmd',
+      'set notermguicolors',
+    }, { env = env_notermguicolors })
+
+    screen:expect({ any = 'TERMINAL' })
+
+    retry(nil, nil, function()
+      server_session = n.connect(server_pipe)
+    end)
+    local _, init_cwd = server_session:request('nvim_call_function', 'getcwd', {})
+    server_session:request('nvim_call_function', 'mkdir', { tmpdir, 'p' })
+    server_session:request('nvim_command', 'cd ' .. tmpdir)
+    local _, after_cd = server_session:request('nvim_call_function', 'getcwd', {})
+    eq(tmpdir, after_cd)
+
+    local _, old_pid = server_session:request('nvim_call_function', 'getpid', {})
+    local _, dir_exists = server_session:request('nvim_call_function', 'isdirectory', { init_cwd })
+    eq(1, dir_exists)
+
+    feed_data(':restart\n')
+
+    -- Wait for the new server to be ready before reconnecting.
+    screen:expect({ any = 'TERMINAL', unchanged = true })
+
+    server_session:close()
+    retry(nil, nil, function()
+      server_session = n.connect(server_pipe)
+    end)
+
+    -- Connected to new proc.
+    local _, new_pid = server_session:request('nvim_call_function', 'getpid', {})
+    t.neq(old_pid, new_pid)
+
+    local _, restart_cwd = server_session:request('nvim_call_function', 'getcwd', {})
+    eq(init_cwd, restart_cwd)
+
+    feed_data(':qall!\r')
+    screen:expect({ any = vim.pesc('[Process exited 0]') })
+  end)
+
+  it('drop [file] argument', function()
+    local server_session
+    finally(function()
+      if server_session then
+        server_session:close()
+      end
+    end)
+    local server_pipe = new_pipename()
+    local screen = tt.setup_child_nvim({
+      '-u',
+      'NONE',
+      '-i',
+      'NONE',
+      '--listen',
+      server_pipe,
+      '--cmd',
+      'set notermguicolors',
+      'Xtest-dropped-file',

comment_data.body: Soryy, i mess it up again, fix it soon! and i wanna ask for some advice about [file] drop, is that ok if i use global var here? Firstly i try to put it in `mparm_T params;` in main.c but it's in the stack, and i try to make it a static var put in somewhere but need to declare getter setter stuff and don't look clean

comment_data.body:
> why not update the existing test on line 495?

Is that mean i should update the current test instead of add a new test `it(drop [file])..`?

That way, noticed that current test skip in windows because of `-` options, should i keep it or not?

And i my test have problem in windows may due to connection establishment. Is there any solution about it, or i just imitate the test exist.

comment_regular_data.body: I don't familiar with the performance in Windows, and have trouble writing the test script, it would be very appreciating if reviewer can give me some advice:)
Okay, update the exist test on line 495, and my new test seem ok. But idk why an unrelated test crash? Is that a flaky test? And the result seem good...

@justinmk,  i update the current test as you wish, and there's two question remain

> command_line_scan could set a hint which :restart can use later
1) i add a global var `argv_is_file`  and set it in `command_line_scan` , i know it's a bad idea, so is there another way i can fetch this context more elegant ? 

2) CICD fail in an unrealted test in Windows `test/functional/terminal/window_spec.lua` ,  and i can't reproduce on my Windows device, The failure is just a +1 line screen shift (content is identical), so it looks flaky, but I'm not sure
```
Expected starts at `++ 7`, actual starts at `++ 8` (cursor line also +1).
Stack:
- `test/functional/ui/screen.lua:536 expect`
- `test/functional/terminal/window_spec.lua:175`
```

labels.description: May contain trace amounts of GMO
labels.name: AI assisted 🤖

title: fix(excmd): restart use the initial directory
